topic.what: Most influential pharmacological text of antiquity, cataloguing ~600 plants including mastic, licorice, wormwood, and aloe for gastric ailments.
topic.how: Systematic plant-by-plant documentation of preparation methods, dosing, and therapeutic indications based on empirical observation.
topic.why_matters: Several Dioscoridean gastric remedies (especially mastic gum) have been validated by modern pharmacological research, making this text a 2000-year-old lead-generation source.

characteristics.value: ~600 plants total, dozens for gastric indications

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of recommended remedies subsequently validated by modern research
characteristics.value: Mastic gum, licorice, aloe all validated; wormwood partially

novelty: First systematic Western pharmacopoeia with gastric-specific indications
Mastic gum recommendation validated 2000 years later against H. pylori
Bridge between Hippocratic dietary therapy and herbal pharmacotherapy
open_problems: Plant identification uncertainty for some entries
Dosing not standardized in modern terms
